description:
Goal
Replace return facts that pretend a ready `Ty` always exists with explicit `ReturnEvidence`. A missing fact means no current evidence has been produced. A produced fact says what was actually learned: known concrete return type, produced unknown/dynamic evidence, or no-return control flow. No consumer should need to guess from `Option<Ty>`, `none`, or `any` whether the callee is absent, still converging, dynamically unknown, or genuinely empty.

Signal
- `FactKey::ReturnType` is gone or renamed to `ReturnEvidence` with a non-`Ty` payload in the semantic store/telemetry/API.
- `ActivationMap` no longer stores `Option<Ty>` as the return contract; it stores explicit evidence and applies recursive finite ascent only to the known-type component.
- A caller whose callee has no current return fact records a current wait/read and does not publish `none` as if the callee returned an empty value set.
- A caller whose callee produced unknown evidence propagates that evidence explicitly until a real boundary chooses dynamic top; the choice site is named and tested.
- A function that genuinely cannot return is represented distinctly from a missing/incomplete return fact.
- Closure/materialization/artifact consumers wait on settled return evidence where complete facts are required.

Strategy
- Write the evidence tests first around the current failure modes: absent callee return, recursive self-call convergence, external/dynamic unknown, and no-return body.
- Introduce the smallest domain type that makes the states explicit, likely at the semantic boundary, with projection helpers named for the boundary that needs a `Ty`.
- Rename/update `world.activation_return`, callsite summaries, telemetry capture, artifact consumers, and tests so they consume evidence deliberately.
- Delete the `unwrap_or_else(|| none_ty(world))` return fallback path and any equivalent `any` fallback that means unknown rather than explicit dynamic semantics.
- Keep fact readiness in the scheduler: absence/current/settled facts remain engine state; `ReturnEvidence` is the semantic payload once a producer has actually produced evidence.

Outro
No `ReturnType`-as-ready-`Ty`, no `Option<Ty>` return slot standing for readiness, no absent-callee-as-`none`, no unknown-callee-as-`any`, no recursive widening outside the evidence domain, and no downstream consumer that infers readiness from a type value.

comments.text: Reordered behind fz-rh2.18.9 because ReturnEvidence should land on top of activation keys that already separate identity partition from input evidence. Otherwise it risks preserving raw-evidence keying under a cleaner return-fact name.

title: Compiler2 semantics: replace ReturnType with ReturnEvidence
